title: Shatteringly Bitter Indian Paneer Curry
description: A date-night Indian dish featuring fresh thyme and a unique bitter flavor profile. Best served with fluffy basmati rice and naan bread.

ingredients.name: Paneer Cheese
ingredients.notes: Extra-firm, non-dairy paneer for a vegan-friendly option

ingredients.name: Fresh Thyme
ingredients.notes: Use fresh thyme for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: Grate the ginger for added depth

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for a stronger flavor

ingredients.name: Milk Thistle
ingredients.notes: Use milk thistle seeds for a unique, bitter flavor

ingredients.name: Tamarind Paste
ingredients.notes: Add a tangy, sour flavor to the curry

ingredients.name: Ghee or Vegan Butter
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil for the best flavor

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion for added texture

ingredients.name: Red Chili Pepper
ingredients.notes: Adjust the heat level to your liking

ingredients.name: Turmeric Powder
ingredients.notes: Add a warm, earthy flavor to the curry

ingredients.name: Coriander Powder
ingredients.notes: Use coriander powder for a slightly sweet flavor

ingredients.name: Cumin Powder
ingredients.notes: Add a warm, earthy flavor to the curry

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust the salt level to your liking

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or vegan butter in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and sauté for another minute.
Add the milk thistle seeds and sauté for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the turmeric powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder, and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the diced red chili pepper and sauté for another minute.
Add the tamarind paste and stir well to combine.
Add the paneer cheese and stir gently to coat with the spice mixture.
Transfer the curry to a blender or food processor and blend until smooth.
Return the curry to the pan and stir in the fresh thyme sprigs.
Season with salt to taste and adjust the heat level as needed.
Serve the curry hot, garnished with additional fresh thyme if desired.
